<Meta of={CardCollectionStories} name="Overview" />

# Card Collection

The card collection component displays multiple cards in a flexible grid or list layout. It supports various card types including posts, events, profiles, resources, and directory listings. Card collections can be configured with different layout modes, featured positioning, and image display options to create organized, scannable content groupings across the site.

## Card Type Variants

The card collection supports the following card types. Each section below is fully interactive — use the controls panel to experiment with layout, featured mode, and image options.

### Post Cards

Standard content cards for blog posts, news, or general content. Supports 3x2 images, headlines, excerpts, and publication date.

<Canvas of={CardCollectionStories.PostCardCollection} />
<Controls of={CardCollectionStories.PostCardCollection} />

### Event Cards

Specialized cards for events with date, format (online/in-person), and CTAs. Supports 3x2 images.

<Canvas of={CardCollectionStories.EventCardCollection} />
<Controls of={CardCollectionStories.EventCardCollection} />

### Profile Cards

People-focused cards with headshots, names, titles, and affiliations. Uses 1x1 images.

<Canvas of={CardCollectionStories.ProfileCardCollection} />
<Controls of={CardCollectionStories.ProfileCardCollection} />

### Directory Listing Cards

Compact profile cards for directory views. Uses the `profile-directory` layout type.

<Canvas of={CardCollectionStories.DirectoryListingCardCollection} />
<Controls of={CardCollectionStories.DirectoryListingCardCollection} />

### Resource Cards

Cards for downloadable resources, documents, or external links. Supports icons, file types, and overlay labels like "Pinned". Toggle the **Portrait** control to switch to the portrait display mode, which renders images at a 1:1.6 ratio.

<Canvas of={CardCollectionStories.ResourceCardCollection} />
<Controls of={CardCollectionStories.ResourceCardCollection} />

## Collection Layouts

The card collection supports multiple layout modes for different content presentation needs:

<div
  style={{
    display: 'grid',
    gridTemplateColumns: 'repeat(2, 1fr)',
    gap: '1.5rem',
    margin: '2rem 0',
  }}
>
  <div
    style={{
      padding: '1.5rem',
      background: '#f0f0f0',
      borderRadius: '8px',
    }}
  >
    <h3 style={{ marginTop: 0, color: '#0053A0' }}>Grid Layout</h3>
    <p style={{ fontSize: '0.95rem', marginBottom: 0 }}>
      Default responsive grid displaying cards in columns. Adapts to viewport
      size for optimal viewing. Best for visual content like posts, events, and
      resources.
    </p>
  </div>

{' '}

<div
  style={{
    padding: '1.5rem',
    background: '#f0f0f0',
    borderRadius: '8px',
  }}
>
  <h3 style={{ marginTop: 0, color: '#0053A0' }}>List Layout</h3>
  <p style={{ fontSize: '0.95rem', marginBottom: 0 }}>
    Single-column vertical list with cards displaying in full width. Ideal for
    detailed content where readability is prioritized. When images are disabled,
    cards flush to left edge.
  </p>
</div>

{' '}

<div
  style={{
    padding: '1.5rem',
    background: '#f0f0f0',
    borderRadius: '8px',
  }}
>
  <h3 style={{ marginTop: 0, color: '#0053A0' }}>Condensed Layout</h3>
  <p style={{ fontSize: '0.95rem', marginBottom: 0 }}>
    Compact display maximizing content density. Suitable for displaying larger
    numbers of items in limited space while maintaining scannability.
  </p>
</div>

  <div
    style={{
      padding: '1.5rem',
      background: '#f0f0f0',
      borderRadius: '8px',
    }}
  >
    <h3 style={{ marginTop: 0, color: '#0053A0' }}>Profile Directory</h3>
    <p style={{ fontSize: '0.95rem', marginBottom: 0 }}>
      Specialized layout for directory listing cards. Optimized grid for
      efficiently displaying multiple people with consistent spacing and
      alignment.
    </p>
  </div>
</div>

## Featured Layout

When <code>card_collection\_\_featured</code> is set to <code>'true'</code>, the first card displays larger and more prominently than subsequent cards. This creates visual hierarchy and draws attention to the most important content. Featured layout works best with 3-4 total cards.

        all devices, touch-optimized for mobile interactions
      </td>
    </tr>
  </tbody>
</table>

## Usage Guidelines

### Image Configuration

- **With images enabled**: Full visual presentation with image aspect ratios appropriate for card type
- **With images disabled**: Text-only presentation optimized for content density
- **List layout without images**: Cards flush to left edge for clean text alignment

### Featured Collections

- Use featured layout to highlight the most important or recent content
- Limit to 3-4 cards total when using featured mode
- Ensure the first card contains the most compelling content

### Card Count Recommendations

- **Grid layout**: 3-12 cards for optimal visual balance
- **List layout**: Any number of cards, particularly effective for 5+ items
- **Condensed layout**: 6-20 cards for maximum density
- **Featured layout**: 3-4 cards total (1 featured + 2-3 standard)

### Content Considerations

- Use consistent card types within a single collection
- Provide meaningful headings when grouping related content
- Ensure image quality is appropriate for the chosen aspect ratio
- Use overlay labels sparingly for special emphasis (e.g., "Pinned", "Featured")

## Related Components

- **Reference Card** - Individual card component for posts, events, profiles, and resources
- **Directory Listing Card** - Compact profile card for directory views
- **Grid** - Base grid system used for layout
- **Heading** - Text component for collection headings

## Visual Regression Testing

For comprehensive testing of all card collection variations including layouts, card types, and feature combinations, see the [Visreg page](?path=/story/organisms-card-collection-visreg--visreg).
